event_name: Sherri Shepherd – Make It Make Sense Tour
blurb: Sherri Shepherd is bringing her stand-up tour to Buckhead Theatre Saturday night and it sounds exactly like what you would expect from her: sharp, funny, unfiltered takes on relationships, pop culture, and everyday life. She has been making people laugh for decades across TV, The View, and now the stage. If you need a night out that does not require a plot summary or a lot of pre-planning, this is it. Grab dinner nearby, walk in, and laugh for two hours. Simple as that.
scoring_notes: Sherri Shepherd has high name recognition with the 40-plus female demographic that makes up a meaningful share of East Cobb newsletter readers. Stand-up comedy at an intimate Buckhead venue is a natural fit for empty nesters and couples looking for a low-friction Saturday night out. Not a one-of-a-kind artist, but the format and familiarity make it an easy yes for this audience.

blurb: If you have never been to the Powder Springs Seafood Festival, this is the weekend to fix that. Three days of fresh seafood, artisan vendors, live entertainment, and outdoor fun at Thurman Springs Park. It is a short drive from East Cobb and the kind of laid-back, spend-the-afternoon setup that works for pretty much everyone. Come Friday evening to beat the crowds, or go Saturday and make a full day of it. Either way, bring your appetite.
scoring_notes: Seafood festivals skew toward adults and established families, which matches this audience well. Powder Springs is close enough to East Cobb to be a natural outing. Price is essentially free to enter with food costs at the reader's discretion. Three-day run gives flexibility, which empty nesters and busy families both appreciate.

blurb: More than 100 local bands, performing on actual neighborhood porches, spread across one of Atlanta's most walkable and beloved neighborhoods for a full Saturday. That is the whole idea behind Virginia Highland Porchfest, and it works every year. You stroll the streets, wander into whatever sounds good, grab food from vendors along the way, and stay as long as you want. There is no stage you have to plant yourself in front of. It is just a great afternoon outside in a neighborhood that knows how to throw a good time. Tickets start at $10.
scoring_notes: Porchfest is a genuinely unique format that the East Cobb audience, educated, culturally engaged, and comfortable in Atlanta, would appreciate. The all-day, come-and-go structure works for both couples and families with teens. Virginia Highland is a natural destination for this demographic on a Saturday. The $10 entry is essentially a non-consideration at this income level. Slight demerit for being Atlanta-centric rather than nearby, but the uniqueness and format make it worth featuring.

blurb: If you have a teenager in the house who plays video games, this one is for them and honestly, you might enjoy it too. DreamHack Atlanta is one of the biggest gaming and esports festivals in the country, and it is happening this weekend at the Georgia World Congress Center. Think Call of Duty and Counter-Strike 2 major tournaments, open play areas, cosplay, an expo floor, and thousands of people who take this stuff seriously. A 3-day family ticket starts at $99 and covers kids under 13 for free. It is a legitimate way to actually do something your kid thinks is cool, which is harder to pull off the older they get.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews toward families with teen and college-age kids, which is the exact demographic DreamHack targets. The event is massive (global esports competition) and not a typical weekend outing, giving it strong uniqueness. Ticket price is very manageable for a $118K median income household. The family ticket structure makes it easy to bring multiple kids. Strong fit for the 'something to do with teens' gap this audience often has.

venue: Holy Transfiguration Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
price: Free admission (food and drinks for purchase)
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back for its 36th year, and it is right in your backyard. This is one of those events that has stuck around for four decades for a simple reason: it delivers every single time. Authentic Greek food made from scratch, traditional music, live dancing, and a crowd that actually has fun. It runs Thursday through Saturday, so you have three chances to get there. Pick a night, bring someone who appreciates a good gyro, and let the spanakopita do the rest.
scoring_notes: Duplicate source reference for same event — see candidate_index 7 notes.

venue: Holy Transfiguration Greek Orthodox Church, Marietta
price: Free admission (food and drinks for purchase)
blurb: The Marietta Greek Festival is back for its 36th year, and it is right in your backyard. This is one of those events that has stuck around for four decades for a simple reason: it delivers every single time. Authentic Greek food made from scratch, traditional music, live dancing, and a crowd that actually has fun. It runs Thursday through Saturday, so you have three chances to get there. Pick a night, bring someone who appreciates a good gyro, and let the spanakopita do the rest.
scoring_notes: Annual event but long-running local institution with strong community identity in East Cobb. Greek food, live music, and outdoor festival format hit perfectly for the mix of established families and empty nesters at median age 42. Zero cost to enter keeps it accessible even though the income level makes price a non-issue. Proximity to East Cobb is a major plus. The 36-year legacy gives it credibility and familiarity that resonates with long-time residents.

blurb: Marcus King is the kind of artist you want to see before everyone else catches up. The Grammy-nominated guitarist and singer is reuniting with his full band at the Fox Theatre Saturday night for a show that pulls from his latest album and the fan favorites that built his reputation. His sound sits right at the crossroads of Southern rock, blues, and soul, and he plays it with the kind of conviction that makes a Fox Theatre show feel like something more than just a concert. There are not many artists doing what he does right now, and the Fox is a perfect room for it. Get tickets before the weekend sneaks up on you.
scoring_notes: Marcus King's musical style, Southern rock and soul, aligns well with the tastes of an educated, 42-median-age East Cobb audience. The Fox Theatre is a beloved Atlanta venue that this demographic is comfortable attending and willing to spend on. Grammy-nominated status gives him enough name recognition without being a mainstream pop act. Strong adult date-night energy. The empty nester segment of this audience is especially likely to respond to a Saturday night concert at an iconic venue.
